designSystem.styleGuidelines:
## Brand & Style
The design system is built upon a concept of visual duality, reflecting the "Good and Evil" narrative of a premium Little Rock smokehouse. It juxtaposes the ethereal, communal "Good" (light, gold, airy layouts) against the intense, charred "Evil" (dark, smoke-filled, high-contrast typography). 

The style is **High-End Editorial**. It avoids traditional "rustic" BBQ clichés in favor of a sophisticated, literary aesthetic found in premium food journals. The interface uses a "Split" motif—clean vertical or horizontal divisions—to showcase the two sides of the culinary experience. Subtle halftone textures are applied to imagery to evoke traditional print techniques and woodblock history.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Fixed Grid** philosophy on desktop with generous, unconventional margins to mimic a high-end magazine. 

*   **Duality Split:** Use a 50/50 vertical split on large screens to contrast content types (e.g., "The Good" ingredients vs. "The Evil" techniques).
*   **Whitespace:** Negative space is treated as a design element, not "empty" space. Large section gaps (120px+) prevent the high-contrast colors from feeling cluttered.
*   **Asymmetry:** Elements should occasionally break the grid or overlap boundaries to create a modern, curated feel.

## Elevation & Depth
This design system rejects standard shadows in favor of **Tonal Layering** and **High-Contrast Outlines**.

*   **Flat Depth:** Hierarchy is established through color blocking rather than blur. Elements are "raised" by switching from Charred Black to Smoke Gray surfaces.
*   **Hard Outlines:** Use 1px solid borders in Gold or Bone White to define interactive containers. 
*   **Glassmorphism (Smoke):** A subtle backdrop-blur (12px) with a 20% opacity black fill is used for navigation overlays to simulate looking through a haze of smoke.

## Components
Consistent styling across the interface:

*   **Buttons:** Primary buttons are solid Divine Gold with Black text, rectangular, and use the `label-bold` type style. Secondary buttons are "Ghost" style with a 1px Bone White border.
*   **Menu Cards:** Use a split layout—item name in Bodoni Moda on the left, price/description in Hanken Grotesk on the right, separated by a thin 1px horizontal rule.
*   **Chips/Tags:** Used for dietary labels (e.g., "Spicy", "GF"). Small, rectangular boxes with Bone White text on a Smoke Gray background.
*   **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only (1px Bone White) with floating labels in Hanken Grotesk to maintain a minimal, editorial look.
*   **Halftone Dividers:** Instead of solid lines, use a recurring halftone gradient pattern to transition between "Good" and "Evil" sections.

theme.designMd:
---
name: Duality & Embers
colors:
  surface: '#131313'
  surface-dim: '#131313'
  surface-bright: '#393939'
  surface-container-lowest: '#0e0e0e'
  surface-container-low: '#1b1c1c'
  surface-container: '#1f2020'
  surface-container-high: '#2a2a2a'
  surface-container-highest: '#353535'
  on-surface: '#e4e2e1'
  on-surface-variant: '#d0c5af'
  inverse-surface: '#e4e2e1'
  inverse-on-surface: '#303030'
  outline: '#99907c'
  outline-variant: '#4d4635'
  surface-tint: '#e9c349'
  primary: '#f2ca50'
  on-primary: '#3c2f00'
  primary-container: '#d4af37'
  on-primary-container: '#554300'
  inverse-primary: '#735c00'
  secondary: '#c9c6c5'
  on-secondary: '#313030'
  secondary-container: '#4a4949'
  on-secondary-container: '#bab8b7'
  tertiary: '#cecece'
  on-tertiary: '#2f3131'
  tertiary-container: '#b2b3b3'
  on-tertiary-container: '#444546'
  error: '#ffb4ab'
  on-error: '#690005'
  error-container: '#93000a'
  on-error-container: '#ffdad6'
  primary-fixed: '#ffe088'
  primary-fixed-dim: '#e9c349'
  on-primary-fixed: '#241a00'
  on-primary-fixed-variant: '#574500'
  secondary-fixed: '#e5e2e1'
  secondary-fixed-dim: '#c9c6c5'
  on-secondary-fixed: '#1c1b1b'
  on-secondary-fixed-variant: '#474646'
  tertiary-fixed: '#e2e2e2'
  tertiary-fixed-dim: '#c6c6c7'
  on-tertiary-fixed: '#1a1c1c'
  on-tertiary-fixed-variant: '#454747'
  background: '#131313'
  on-background: '#e4e2e1'
  surface-variant: '#353535'
typography:
  display-lg:
    fontFamily: Bodoni Moda
    fontSize: 72px
    fontWeight: '800'
    lineHeight: '1.1'
    letterSpacing: -0.02em
  display-lg-mobile:
    fontFamily: Bodoni Moda
    fontSize: 48px
    fontWeight: '800'
    lineHeight: '1.1'
  headline-xl:
    fontFamily: Bodoni Moda
    fontSize: 48px
    fontWeight: '700'
    lineHeight: '1.2'
  headline-lg:
    fontFamily: Bodoni Moda
    fontSize: 32px
    fontWeight: '700'
    lineHeight: '1.3'
  body-lg:
    fontFamily: Hanken Grotesk
    fontSize: 18px
    fontWeight: '400'
    lineHeight: '1.6'
  body-md:
    fontFamily: Hanken Grotesk
    fontSize: 16px
    fontWeight: '400'
    lineHeight: '1.5'
  label-bold:
    fontFamily: Hanken Grotesk
    fontSize: 14px
    fontWeight: '700'
    lineHeight: '1.0'
    letterSpacing: 0.1em
  caption:
    fontFamily: Hanken Grotesk
    fontSize: 12px
    fontWeight: '500'
    lineHeight: '1.4'
    letterSpacing: 0.05em
spacing:
  unit: 8px
  container-max: 1280px
  gutter: 24px
  margin-mobile: 16px
  margin-desktop: 64px
  section-gap: 120px
---

## Brand & Style
The design system is built upon a concept of visual duality, reflecting the "Good and Evil" narrative of a premium Little Rock smokehouse. It juxtaposes the ethereal, communal "Good" (light, gold, airy layouts) against the intense, charred "Evil" (dark, smoke-filled, high-contrast typography). 

The style is **High-End Editorial**. It avoids traditional "rustic" BBQ clichés in favor of a sophisticated, literary aesthetic found in premium food journals. The interface uses a "Split" motif—clean vertical or horizontal divisions—to showcase the two sides of the culinary experience. Subtle halftone textures are applied to imagery to evoke traditional print techniques and woodblock history.

## Colors
The palette is a high-contrast study in light and shadow. 

*   **Charred Black (#0D0D0D):** The foundation. Used for backgrounds to represent the "Evil" side—intensity, smoke, and the char of the pit.
*   **Bone White (#F5F5F5):** The "Good" side. Used for high-readability canvases, evoking the purity of ingredients and communal light.
*   **Divine Gold (#D4AF37):** A premium accent used sparingly for iconography, borders, and call-to-actions, representing the "heavenly" flavor and the glow of embers.
*   **Smoke Gray (#333333):** Used for secondary text, subtle dividers, and depth-building in dark mode.

The design system utilizes a "Hard-Switch" approach where sections are either predominantly Bone White with Black text or Charred Black with Gold/White text, reinforcing the duality.

## Typography
Typography is the primary vehicle for the brand’s authoritative voice. 

**Bodoni Moda** is used for all headlines. Its high contrast between thick and thin strokes provides a classic editorial feel that mirrors the "cut" of a blade and the precision of the smokehouse. 

**Hanken Grotesk** serves as the utilitarian balance. It is clean, contemporary, and highly legible, ensuring that menu descriptions and logistics remain accessible despite the bold aesthetic.

Hierarchy is enforced through extreme scale—large display serifs paired with small, tracked-out uppercase sans-serif labels.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Fixed Grid** philosophy on desktop with generous, unconventional margins to mimic a high-end magazine. 

*   **Duality Split:** Use a 50/50 vertical split on large screens to contrast content types (e.g., "The Good" ingredients vs. "The Evil" techniques).
*   **Whitespace:** Negative space is treated as a design element, not "empty" space. Large section gaps (120px+) prevent the high-contrast colors from feeling cluttered.
*   **Asymmetry:** Elements should occasionally break the grid or overlap boundaries to create a modern, curated feel.

## Elevation & Depth
This design system rejects standard shadows in favor of **Tonal Layering** and **High-Contrast Outlines**.

*   **Flat Depth:** Hierarchy is established through color blocking rather than blur. Elements are "raised" by switching from Charred Black to Smoke Gray surfaces.
*   **Hard Outlines:** Use 1px solid borders in Gold or Bone White to define interactive containers. 
*   **Glassmorphism (Smoke):** A subtle backdrop-blur (12px) with a 20% opacity black fill is used for navigation overlays to simulate looking through a haze of smoke.

## Shapes
The shape language is **Sharp (0)**. 

To maintain the authoritative and "cut" nature of the brand, no rounded corners are permitted. Buttons, input fields, images, and cards must all utilize 90-degree angles. This geometric rigidity reinforces the professional, "no-cliché" editorial stance of this design system.

## Components
Consistent styling across the interface:

*   **Buttons:** Primary buttons are solid Divine Gold with Black text, rectangular, and use the `label-bold` type style. Secondary buttons are "Ghost" style with a 1px Bone White border.
*   **Menu Cards:** Use a split layout—item name in Bodoni Moda on the left, price/description in Hanken Grotesk on the right, separated by a thin 1px horizontal rule.
*   **Chips/Tags:** Used for dietary labels (e.g., "Spicy", "GF"). Small, rectangular boxes with Bone White text on a Smoke Gray background.
*   **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only (1px Bone White) with floating labels in Hanken Grotesk to maintain a minimal, editorial look.
*   **Halftone Dividers:** Instead of solid lines, use a recurring halftone gradient pattern to transition between "Good" and "Evil" sections.

screens.name: projects/9132108788539206898/screens/83324f1ddeb54be6b1bc7c792b3b10de
screens.prompt:
A complete, high-fidelity DESKTOP landing page for "Good and Evil BBQ" in Little Rock, AR. Design Theme: "Duality & Embers" - Deep charred blacks (#131313) and warm bone whites, accented by Divine Gold (#d4af37). Use Bodoni Moda for authoritative, editorial headlines. Layout Strategy: Asymmetric, narrative-driven editorial structure. Move between dark and light sections to reflect the "Good" (community/soul) and "Evil" (intense smoke/char). Sections: 1. Header: Use the TopAppBar component with logo {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_4}}, phone (501) 214-8040, and "Order Now" action. 2. Hero: "The Sacred Art of Smoke." Use high-end imagery {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_6}} (brisket slicing). Narrative copy about fire, time, and transcendence. 3. #credibility: "Little Rock's Best Kept Secret." Asymmetric grid highlighting heritage and Arkansan choice. 4. Services/Offerings: "Shared Plates, Common Ground." Use imagery {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_5}} (community pit). Highlight Signature Brisket, Community Pit Catering, and The Fallen Ribs. 5. About: "Eric Love & The Smoke Legacy." A deep-dive into the history of the Springer Boulevard pit. 6. Visit: #visit section with location (2616 Springer Boulevard), hours, and the required map slot:
Map Visualization Loading...
. 7. Contact: "Reserve Your Table." High-contrast form with validation UI states. 8. Footer: Use the Footer component with address, phone, and social links. Technical Requirements: - Full semantic HTML5/CSS/JS. - Responsive design. - Smooth scroll navigation. - No placeholder text; use the provided brand details. - Use CSS custom properties for the palette. ## Available Images You MUST include the following images in this design. You must reference them by putting their exact placeholder IDs in `src` attributes or CSS `background-image` rules. Do NOT use external URLs or generate new placeholders. Preserve these IDs exactly as written. Read each image's Description to understand its subject, intended section, and visual role. - {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_4}} Description: Brand logo. - Primary color: #d4af37 - Font: bodoniModa - Mode: dark - Roundness: rounded-lg Dimensions: 400x160 Aspect Ratio: 2.50 Colors: #333333, #D4AF37, #F5F5F5 - {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_5}} Description: A soulful, warm photograph of a weathered wooden BBQ pit in Little Rock, Arkansas at sunrise. Soft, divine gold light filtering through wood smoke. Atmospheric, community-focused, nostalgic but premium. 8k resolution. Dimensions: 1264x848 Aspect Ratio: 1.49 - {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_6}} Description: A high-end, editorial photograph of premium smoked brisket being sliced. The lighting is cinematic and low-key, highlighting the "smoke ring" and the glistening texture of the meat against a charred black background. Divine gold light catching the smoke. No people. 8k resolution, professional food photography. Dimensions: 1376x768 Aspect Ratio: 1.79

*   **Charred Black (#0D0D0D):** The foundation. Used for backgrounds to represent the "Evil" side—intensity, smoke, and the char of the pit.
*   **Bone White (#F5F5F5):** The "Good" side. Used for high-readability canvases, evoking the purity of ingredients and communal light.
*   **Divine Gold (#D4AF37):** A premium accent used sparingly for iconography, borders, and call-to-actions, representing the "heavenly" flavor and the glow of embers.
*   **Smoke Gray (#333333):** Used for secondary text, subtle dividers, and depth-building in dark mode.

The design system utilizes a "Hard-Switch" approach where sections are either predominantly Bone White with Black text or Charred Black with Gold/White text, reinforcing the duality.

## Typography
Typography is the primary vehicle for the brand’s authoritative voice. 

**Bodoni Moda** is used for all headlines. Its high contrast between thick and thin strokes provides a classic editorial feel that mirrors the "cut" of a blade and the precision of the smokehouse. 

**Hanken Grotesk** serves as the utilitarian balance. It is clean, contemporary, and highly legible, ensuring that menu descriptions and logistics remain accessible despite the bold aesthetic.

Hierarchy is enforced through extreme scale—large display serifs paired with small, tracked-out uppercase sans-serif labels.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Fixed Grid** philosophy on desktop with generous, unconventional margins to mimic a high-end magazine. 

*   **Duality Split:** Use a 50/50 vertical split on large screens to contrast content types (e.g., "The Good" ingredients vs. "The Evil" techniques).
*   **Whitespace:** Negative space is treated as a design element, not "empty" space. Large section gaps (120px+) prevent the high-contrast colors from feeling cluttered.
*   **Asymmetry:** Elements should occasionally break the grid or overlap boundaries to create a modern, curated feel.

## Elevation & Depth
This design system rejects standard shadows in favor of **Tonal Layering** and **High-Contrast Outlines**.

*   **Flat Depth:** Hierarchy is established through color blocking rather than blur. Elements are "raised" by switching from Charred Black to Smoke Gray surfaces.
*   **Hard Outlines:** Use 1px solid borders in Gold or Bone White to define interactive containers. 
*   **Glassmorphism (Smoke):** A subtle backdrop-blur (12px) with a 20% opacity black fill is used for navigation overlays to simulate looking through a haze of smoke.

## Shapes
The shape language is **Sharp (0)**. 

To maintain the authoritative and "cut" nature of the brand, no rounded corners are permitted. Buttons, input fields, images, and cards must all utilize 90-degree angles. This geometric rigidity reinforces the professional, "no-cliché" editorial stance of this design system.

## Components
Consistent styling across the interface:

*   **Buttons:** Primary buttons are solid Divine Gold with Black text, rectangular, and use the `label-bold` type style. Secondary buttons are "Ghost" style with a 1px Bone White border.
*   **Menu Cards:** Use a split layout—item name in Bodoni Moda on the left, price/description in Hanken Grotesk on the right, separated by a thin 1px horizontal rule.
*   **Chips/Tags:** Used for dietary labels (e.g., "Spicy", "GF"). Small, rectangular boxes with Bone White text on a Smoke Gray background.
*   **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only (1px Bone White) with floating labels in Hanken Grotesk to maintain a minimal, editorial look.
*   **Halftone Dividers:** Instead of solid lines, use a recurring halftone gradient pattern to transition between "Good" and "Evil" sections.

screens.name: projects/9132108788539206898/screens/1544be55b9394000a364ca3529743143
screens.prompt:
Add atmospheric motion and interactivity to the screen:
1. Smoke Particles: In the dark hero and "Evil" sections, add a vanilla JS particle system that creates subtle, slow-rising smoke embers using div elements with CSS blurs.
2. Scroll Reveals: Implement an IntersectionObserver to trigger 'fade-in-up' animations as the user scrolls through the editorial sections.
3. Mouse Tracking: Add a subtle 'Divine Gold' glow effect that follows the cursor, restricted to the dark sections of the page.
4. Smooth Scrolling: Ensure all navigation links use smooth scroll behavior.
5. Form Interactivity: Add simple JS validation UI for the reservation form (e.g., showing a gold border on success or subtle shake on error).

Keep the core layout and assets {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_4}}, {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_5}}, and {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_6}} exactly as they are. Use vanilla JS and CSS only.

## Brand & Style
The design system is built upon a concept of visual duality, reflecting the "Good and Evil" narrative of a premium Little Rock smokehouse. It juxtaposes the ethereal, communal "Good" (light, gold, airy layouts) against the intense, charred "Evil" (dark, smoke-filled, high-contrast typography). 

The style is **High-End Editorial**. It avoids traditional "rustic" BBQ clichés in favor of a sophisticated, literary aesthetic found in premium food journals. The interface uses a "Split" motif—clean vertical or horizontal divisions—to showcase the two sides of the culinary experience. Subtle halftone textures are applied to imagery to evoke traditional print techniques and woodblock history.

## Colors
The palette is a high-contrast study in light and shadow. 

*   **Charred Black (#0D0D0D):** The foundation. Used for backgrounds to represent the "Evil" side—intensity, smoke, and the char of the pit.
*   **Bone White (#F5F5F5):** The "Good" side. Used for high-readability canvases, evoking the purity of ingredients and communal light.
*   **Divine Gold (#D4AF37):** A premium accent used sparingly for iconography, borders, and call-to-actions, representing the "heavenly" flavor and the glow of embers.
*   **Smoke Gray (#333333):** Used for secondary text, subtle dividers, and depth-building in dark mode.

The design system utilizes a "Hard-Switch" approach where sections are either predominantly Bone White with Black text or Charred Black with Gold/White text, reinforcing the duality.

## Typography
Typography is the primary vehicle for the brand’s authoritative voice. 

**Bodoni Moda** is used for all headlines. Its high contrast between thick and thin strokes provides a classic editorial feel that mirrors the "cut" of a blade and the precision of the smokehouse. 

**Hanken Grotesk** serves as the utilitarian balance. It is clean, contemporary, and highly legible, ensuring that menu descriptions and logistics remain accessible despite the bold aesthetic.

Hierarchy is enforced through extreme scale—large display serifs paired with small, tracked-out uppercase sans-serif labels.

## Layout & Spacing

*   **Duality Split:** Use a 50/50 vertical split on large screens to contrast content types (e.g., "The Good" ingredients vs. "The Evil" techniques).
*   **Whitespace:** Negative space is treated as a design element, not "empty" space. Large section gaps (120px+) prevent the high-contrast colors from feeling cluttered.
*   **Asymmetry:** Elements should occasionally break the grid or overlap boundaries to create a modern, curated feel.

## Elevation & Depth
This design system rejects standard shadows in favor of **Tonal Layering** and **High-Contrast Outlines**.

*   **Flat Depth:** Hierarchy is established through color blocking rather than blur. Elements are "raised" by switching from Charred Black to Smoke Gray surfaces.
*   **Hard Outlines:** Use 1px solid borders in Gold or Bone White to define interactive containers. 
*   **Glassmorphism (Smoke):** A subtle backdrop-blur (12px) with a 20% opacity black fill is used for navigation overlays to simulate looking through a haze of smoke.

## Shapes
The shape language is **Sharp (0)**. 

To maintain the authoritative and "cut" nature of the brand, no rounded corners are permitted. Buttons, input fields, images, and cards must all utilize 90-degree angles. This geometric rigidity reinforces the professional, "no-cliché" editorial stance of this design system.

## Components
Consistent styling across the interface:

*   **Buttons:** Primary buttons are solid Divine Gold with Black text, rectangular, and use the `label-bold` type style. Secondary buttons are "Ghost" style with a 1px Bone White border.
*   **Menu Cards:** Use a split layout—item name in Bodoni Moda on the left, price/description in Hanken Grotesk on the right, separated by a thin 1px horizontal rule.
*   **Chips/Tags:** Used for dietary labels (e.g., "Spicy", "GF"). Small, rectangular boxes with Bone White text on a Smoke Gray background.
*   **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only (1px Bone White) with floating labels in Hanken Grotesk to maintain a minimal, editorial look.
*   **Halftone Dividers:** Instead of solid lines, use a recurring halftone gradient pattern to transition between "Good" and "Evil" sections.

screens.title: Good and Evil BBQ - Interactive Landing Page

outputComponents.text:
I've completed the production-ready desktop landing page for **Good and Evil BBQ**. 

This design brings the "Duality & Embers" concept to life through a high-contrast editorial layout that balances the "Good" (community/soul) and the "Evil" (mastery of the pit).

### Key Features:
*   **Atmospheric Motion:** I've added a custom JavaScript smoke particle system to the dark sections and subtle scroll-triggered reveals to enhance the narrative flow.
*   **Editorial Narrative:** The layout moves away from generic grids, using asymmetric sections and cinematic photography (A soulful, warm photograph of a weathered wooden BBQ pit in Little Rock, Arkansas at sunrise. Soft, divine gold light filtering through wood smoke. Atmospheric, community-focused, nostalgic but premium. 8k resolution., A high-end, editorial photograph of premium smoked brisket being sliced. The lighting is cinematic and low-key, highlighting the "smoke ring" and the glistening texture of the meat against a charred black background. Divine gold light catching the smoke. No people. 8k resolution, professional food photography.) to tell the story of the Little Rock smokehouse.
*   **Production-Ready Structure:**
    *   **Fixed Navigation:** Fast access to ordering and contact.
    *   **Interactive #Visit Section:** Includes the required map slot and location details.
    *   **Custom Contact UI:** A high-contrast reservation form with interactive states.
    *   **Responsive Fidelity:** Optimized for desktop with a fluid structure that maintains its editorial feel.

The final interactive version is now live on your canvas.

outputComponents.suggestion: Make the smoke effect more intense
